posts.post_caption:
Yesterday we celebrated pharmacists.

Today let's talk about what they actually need.

The average community pharmacist works 47 hours a week.
90% say their workload has increased in the last 2 years.
Funding per item has decreased in real terms since 2015.
Yet pharmacy now delivers more clinical services than ever before.

The question isn't whether pharmacists are good enough. It's whether the system supports them enough.

We built Titan because the tools pharmacists were given didn't match the job they were being asked to do.

That hasn't changed. But it should.

#WorldPharmacistsDay #PharmacyFunding #CommunityPharmacy #TitanPMR

1,400+ pharmacies.
Every one of them run by someone who chose this profession.

Not because it was easy. Because it mattered.

Today we're not talking about workflows or automation or batch dispensing.

We're talking about the people.

The pharmacist who stayed late because a patient didn't understand their new medication.
The dispenser who spotted the interaction the GP missed.
The counter staff who calmed down the parent at 5:55pm on a Friday.

World Pharmacists Day isn't a marketing opportunity. It's a reminder that this profession holds communities together.

Thank you. All of you.

posts.idea:
7-slide carousel — The Operating System Problem
Focus: Strategic future-proofing / why bolt-on services fail

OPEN LOOP MECHANISM: Slide 1 names the mismatch — service-led ambition on dispensing infrastructure. The loop is: why does that not work, and what does the right foundation look like? The carousel earns the answer by walking through exactly what is missing and why adding tools does not fix it.

---

SLIDE 1 — Hook
"Most pharmacy owners are trying to run a service-led business on a system built for dispensing volume."
Line 2: "That is not a strategy problem."
Line 3: "It is an infrastructure problem."
Background: near-black (#1a1a1a). Anton, white. Three lines with deliberate visual pauses between them. The reframe lands on the third line.

SLIDE 2 — Acknowledge the PMR
"A dispensing-first PMR is optimised for one thing."
"Moving items through a dispensary accurately and quickly."
"That is what it was designed for. That is what it does well."
Background: near-black. This slide is important — it does not attack the PMR, it scopes it correctly. That makes the argument credible.

SLIDE 3 — What a service-led pharmacy actually needs
Clean list, each item one line, no bullets:
Consultation workflows.
Clinical pathways.
Prescribing integrated with the consultation.
Payment connected to booking.
Automatic follow-up.
Governance as a by-product of the work.
Small header: "A dispensing PMR does not have these. Not because it is bad software. Because it was built for a different job."
Background: near-black. This list is the argument.

SLIDE 4 — Where most owners get stuck
"This is where most service-led pharmacies get stuck."
They build services on top of a system that was never designed to support them.
Booking platform. Consultation tool. Prescribing software. Payment system.
Each one works. None of them connect.
Background: near-black. The four separate tools should be shown visually — floating, disconnected.

SLIDE 5 — The fragility
"The result is a service operation that is technically running but operationally fragile."
"It scales to a point, then breaks."
"Not because the pharmacist is not good enough. Because the foundation was never built for this."
Background: near-black. This is the cliffhanger — the reader asks: what is the right foundation?

SLIDE 6 — The answer
"The pharmacies running services at scale without the associated chaos have one thing in common."
"They are running on infrastructure that was built for clinical services, not retrofitted for them."
Background: Titanverse gradient (#FF65DD to #B752FF). Earned reveal — no product name, the colour is the signal.

SLIDE 7 — Takeaway
"There is no version of a serious clinical pharmacy operation that runs sustainably on a dispensing OS with services bolted on."
"At some point, the infrastructure has to match the ambition."
Background: Titanverse gradient. Confident, not motivational. This is a conclusion, not a rallying cry.

---

DESIGN RULES:
— Anton for all headline slides
— SF Pro for list/supporting text
— Max 15 words on image per slide (slide 3 list is the exception — each item is short)
— Near-black (#1a1a1a) slides 1—5
— Titanverse gradient slides 6—7
— Slide 4 visual: four disconnected tool icons floating without connection. Slide 6: same four items, now connected into one flow. That contrast is the visual payoff.
— No slogans. No motivational poster energy.
— 1:1 square aspect ratio
posts.post_caption:
Most pharmacy owners are trying to run a service-led business on a system built for dispensing volume.

That is not a strategy problem. It is an infrastructure one.

A dispensing PMR is optimised for moving items through a dispensary. That is exactly what it was designed to do. It does it well.

But a service-led pharmacy needs consultation workflows, integrated prescribing, connected payment, automatic follow-up, and governance that happens inside the work rather than after it. A dispensing PMR does not have those things. Not because it is bad software. Because it was built for a different job.

Adding a booking platform, a consultation tool, a prescribing system and a payment portal does not solve the infrastructure problem. It creates five separate tools where one connected system should be.

There is no version of a serious clinical pharmacy operation that runs sustainably on infrastructure that was never designed for it.

#Titanverse #PharmacyServices #ClinicalPharmacy #PharmacyBusiness
posts.name: TV_OperatingSystemProblem_Carousel

Sajid to camera telling Kanav/Winrosh Pharmacy story.

Quote: "One of the pharmacies that always sticks in my mind — Kanav, Winrosh Pharmacy. Bought the pharmacy, did zero items. Within six, seven months, because he adopted a new way of working — not only click to always fill — he's grown to six, seven thousand items. Started from zero, chose the right digital partner, built a profitable and thriving business. And actually the community now has a thriving pharmacy that wasn't there before."

posts.post_caption:
Zero items. Brand new pharmacy. No history.

That was Kanav when he bought Winrosh Pharmacy.

Within six months, he'd adopted a completely new way of working. Click to always fill. Digital-first from day one.

Today? Six to seven thousand items. A profitable, thriving business.

And a community that finally has a pharmacy looking after them that wasn't there before.

These are the stories we hear all the time. And we're proud to be part of them.

Sajid Ramzan, Chief Sales Officer

#TitanPMR #PharmacyGrowth #CommunityPharmacy #NewPharmacy

posts.idea:
7-slide carousel — "Random Facts About Pharmacists" (Semrush's personality-building format)

CONCEPT: Each slide is a "fact" that's actually a relatable workplace truth. Self-deprecating humour builds brand affinity.

Slide 1: Photo/illustration of confused/exasperated pharmacist face
Large text: "Random facts you probably don't know about pharmacists →"
Subtext: Titan PMR watermark small in corner

Slide 2: Large text, centred:
"Contrary to popular belief, pharmacists' job is ✨patient care✨"
Smaller text below: "Not counting, labelling, chasing GPs, fixing EPS errors, answering the same question 47 times..."

Slide 3: Large text:
"Statistically, 0% of pharmacists have ever had a lunch break that wasn't interrupted"

Slide 4: Large text:
"There have been no recorded instances of a pharmacist enjoying a 'quick chat' with a rep at 4:55pm"

Slide 5: Large text:
"The number of Post-it notes on their monitor is directly correlated to how close they are to burnout"

Slide 6: Large text:
"Pharmacists never recommend their own pharmacy to friends because they know what it's actually like back there"

Slide 7: Large text:
"It's scientifically proven that saying 'it's just a simple repeat' means it absolutely isn't"

Optional Slide 8: End card with Titan PMR logo
"We get it. 💊" / titanpmr.com

DESIGN NOTES:
- Bold, clean sans-serif typography
- Dark background throughout
- Large text that's readable at small sizes
- Subtle Titan watermark on each slide (not obnoxious)
- Consistent layout slide-to-slide
- Real human face on slide 1 creates connection

TONE: Self-deprecating, funny, insider knowledge. Shows Titan understands pharmacy life.
posts.post_caption:

Stock control isn't just about efficiency. It's about cash flow, confidence, and control.

posts.idea:
5-slide carousel — Batch Flow theme

Slide 1: "BATCH VS CHAOS" — Split visual: organised batch picking vs cluttered baskets

Slide 2: "THE TRADITIONAL WAY" — Pick one script at a time, walk shelves repeatedly, mental load increases, speed costs accuracy

Slide 3: "THE BATCH FLOW WAY" — Pick one product for many patients, one shelf run, redistribute with barcode scanning, scale without sacrificing safety

Slide 4: Key stat: "Batch Flow pharmacies process 2-3x the volume with the same team."

Slide 5: CTA — "Chaos doesn't scale. Systems do." Titan PMR logo. titanpmr.com
posts.post_caption:
Two ways to dispense 300 items.

The traditional way:
Pick one script at a time.
Walk the shelves 300 times.
Hope nothing goes wrong.

The Batch Flow way:
Pick Metformin for all 47 patients at once.
One shelf run.
Redistribute with barcode scanning.

Batch Flow pharmacies process 2-3x the volume with the same team.

Chaos doesn't scale. Systems do.

👉 titanpmr.com

#TitanPMR #BatchFlow #PharmacyEfficiency
posts.name: TITAN_BatchVsChaos

posts.idea:

posts.idea:
6-slide carousel — Data & Reporting theme

Slide 1: Bold hook — "WHAT IF YOUR PMR COULD ANSWER THE QUESTIONS THAT ACTUALLY MATTER?" Dark background, large white text.

Slide 2: "Not this:" with typical dashboard metrics: fridge log tick, CD register tick, barcode %. Boring checklist.

Slide 3: "But this:" with real owner questions:
- How much money did I make this month?
- Which branch is losing me money?
- Am I GPhC ready right now?
- Where are my patients coming from?

Slide 4: "The old way" — pie charts, bar graphs that look busy but say nothing.

Slide 5: "The Titan way" — Question-driven analytics. You ask, Titan answers.

Slide 6: CTA — "Data should answer questions, not create more." Titan PMR logo. titanpmr.com
posts.post_caption:
Your PMR gives you 50 graphs.

But can it answer one simple question?

"How much money did I make this month at my Oakley branch?"

Most dashboards show you fridge logs, barcode percentages, CD registers.
Tick, tick, tick.

Nobody's lying awake at 2am thinking about their fridge log.

They're thinking:
• Am I losing money?
• Can I afford to hire?
• If GPhC walked in tomorrow, am I ready?

Data should answer questions. Not create more of them.

👉 titanpmr.com

#TitanPMR #PharmacyData #PharmacyAnalytics #CommunityPharmacy

posts.idea:
7-slide carousel — The Relationship That Never Happened
Focus: Private care economics and CRM

OPEN LOOP MECHANISM: Slide 1 opens the loop with a specific, recognisable scenario — private patient pays, leaves, never returns. The loop is: why does that keep happening, and what changes it? Titanverse arrives on slide 6 as the infrastructure that closes the gap, not as a product pitch.

---

SLIDE 1 — Hook
"A patient pays £55 for a private weight management consultation."
"They leave."
"You never speak to them again."
Three lines. Each on its own row. Generous space between them. The third line lands as the problem — the reader asks: is that wrong? Swipe to find out.
Background: near-black (#1a1a1a). Anton, white.

SLIDE 2 — Reframe
"That is not a bad outcome."
"That is the default."
Small header: "In most pharmacies, a private consultation ends when the patient walks out."
No follow-up pathway. No recall system. No next appointment.
Background: near-black. This slide reframes the problem as structural, not personal. The reader recognises their own operation.

SLIDE 3 — Contrast
Split layout. Two columns:
Left: "Other clinical settings" — care record, follow-up booked, reason to return.
Right: "Pharmacy" — transaction ends at the counter.
Same patient. Different infrastructure. Different outcome.
Background: near-black. The visual contrast IS the argument.

SLIDE 4 — Why it happens
"That is not a patient care problem."
"It is a systems problem."
The tools most pharmacies use for private services were not built to track the patient beyond the appointment.
Background: near-black. Blame the infrastructure, not the pharmacist.

SLIDE 5 — The value argument (no fabricated stats)
Three parallel statements in sequence:
"A patient who comes back for review is more valuable than a new one."
"A patient who books travel vaccines because someone followed up is more valuable than a new one."
"A patient who hears from you before their annual review is more valuable than a new one."
All three require a system that owns the relationship after the consultation.
Background: near-black shifting to gradient. The rhythm of the three statements builds weight.

SLIDE 6 — Mechanism reveal
"When follow-up, re-booking triggers and long-term care tracking are built into the same platform as the consultation, the relationship does not end at the door."
"It continues because the system continues it."
Background: full Titanverse gradient (#FF65DD to #B752FF). This is the earned reveal.

SLIDE 7 — Takeaway
"Private services are not just about the appointment."
"They are about whether the patient comes back."
"That only happens when something is looking after the relationship."
Background: Titanverse gradient. One thought per line. Restrained. No hard sell.

---

DESIGN RULES:
— Anton for all statement slides
— SF Pro for supporting text
— Max 15 words on image per slide
— Near-black (#1a1a1a) slides 1—5
— Titanverse gradient (#FF65DD to #B752FF) slides 6—7
— Slide 3 split layout is the visual centrepiece — give it room
— No fabricated statistics anywhere
— No Titanverse logo on image
— 1:1 square aspect ratio
posts.post_caption:
A patient pays for a private weight management consultation.
They leave.
You never speak to them again.

That is not a failure. That is the default.

In most pharmacies, the private consultation ends the moment the patient walks out. No follow-up. No recall. No reason built into the system for them to return.

In any other clinical setting, that patient would have a care record, a follow-up booked, and a pathway back to you.

A returning patient costs less to retain than a new one costs to acquire. Every private service you offer is a potential long-term clinical relationship. Most pharmacies are leaving that on the table because the tools they use were not built to own what happens after the appointment.

#Titanverse #PrivateHealthcare #PharmacyServices #PharmacyBusiness
posts.name: TV_SilentLead_CRM_Carousel

posts.idea: Stat graphic. Clean, stark, high contrast. Two large numbers dominate: '2,500' then an arrow or year line, then '25,000'. Titan PMR blue gradient (#2DA7FF to #2D40FF) on the larger number. Dark background. Supporting text below: 'Items dispensed per month. Malpas Pharmacy, Newport. 2003 → 2026.' Attribution: Geoff Thomas, Titan PMR customer since 2020. Titan PMR logo bottom right. Minimal. Let the numbers do the work.
posts.post_caption:
2,500 items a month in 2003.

25,000 items a month now.

Same pharmacist. Same Newport community. A completely different way of working.

Geoff Thomas has run Malpas Pharmacy for 22 years. The growth happened gradually — but the change in how pharmacy operates was not gradual at all.

The old model: one pharmacist at the end of everything. Every prescription cleared through him. Final check. Nothing moved without him.

At 2,500 items, that's manageable. At 25,000, it would be impossible.

With Titan PMR, prescriptions are clinically checked at the front and released. The system scans everything. It flags the exceptions. The pharmacist is freed to run services, develop the team, and grow the business.

Malpas Brook — a second site — opened in 2019. Already doing 13,000 items a month and still growing. Planning permission submitted to extend the building.

You don't scale from 2,500 to 38,000 items across two sites on the old model.

#TitanPMR #PharmacyGrowth #IndependentPharmacy #CommunityPharmacy #PharmacyOwner
posts.name: TITAN_Geoff_Scale_Stat

posts.idea: THOUGHT LEADERSHIP — CONSULTATION ECONOMY (TV Industry I6). Single image with editorial/insight feel. Headline: The consultation economy is here. Stat callouts in the visual: Pharmacy First ~15 per consultation | Travel Health 50-150+ | Weight Management: recurring monthly. Clean dark background, teal accents for the numbers. No product UI, no Titanverse feature mentions. Reads as industry insight, not product marketing. Titanverse logo subtle bottom corner. Saveable format. NOTE: Verify current Pharmacy First fees from NHSE before publishing. Travel health and weight management figures are industry-typical ranges.
posts.post_caption:
Here's something most pharmacy owners haven't done the maths on.

A single Pharmacy First consultation takes roughly 10-15 minutes and earns about £15.

A private travel health consultation takes 20-30 minutes and can earn £50-150+.

A weight management programme earns recurring monthly revenue from a single patient.

The pharmacies building real income from services aren't doing more. They're doing different.

They've moved from "we offer consultations" to "we run a clinical business."

The difference isn't ambition. It's infrastructure. Booking. Payments. Follow-ups. Clinical records. Compliance. All connected, not cobbled.

The consultation economy is here. The question is whether your setup can support it.

#Titanverse #PharmacyServices #ClinicalServices #PharmacyBusiness

posts.idea: Multi-image photo dump. 12 images in this order: DSC01139 (Geoff's hands holding old photos, backlit window) → DSC01141 (hands holding photos of two men smiling in dispensary) → DSC01144 (printed photo of old Malpas storefront) → DSC01136 (printed photo of old chaotic dispensary, red/green baskets, paper slips) → DSC01137 (printed photo of old pharmacy interior with wooden shelving sections) → DSC01138 (printed photo of building with scaffolding) → DSC01131 (current Malpas exterior, clean and modern) → DSC01125 (woman in striped jumper at organised dispensary workstation) → DSC01123 (woman with grey hair dispensing, colour-coded baskets) → DSC01124 (dispensary overview, shelves of yellow baskets, organised workspace) → DSC01126 (two staff walking through dispensary corridor, Malpas Pharmacy uniforms) → DSC01127 (staff member on phone at dispensary desk, colour-coded shelves behind her). Arc: nostalgia opening, the past in print, the contrast of before and now, the people and systems of today.
posts.post_caption:
Geoff Thomas has owned Malpas Pharmacy in Newport since 2003.

He brought these photos to our visit last week. Printed. Worn at the edges.

The old dispensary. The old shopfront. A building with scaffolding where the new one would eventually go. Community events from the early days. A pharmacy in the process of becoming something.

He started with 2,500 items a month. A single site. One pharmacist doing the final check on everything that left the building.

He's now doing 25,000 to 27,000 items a month at Malpas. A second pharmacy, Malpas Brook, opened in 2019 and is already doing 12,000 to 13,000 items and still growing. Forty staff across both sites. Planning permission submitted last week to extend the Brook building because the business has outgrown it.

He was one of the first pharmacists in Wales to switch to Titan PMR. He went live at Brook in December 2019, Malpas in 2020.

"You don't know there's anything better until you take that step," he told us. "When you're in an old system, that's all you know."

What changed: the pharmacist is no longer the funnel. Prescriptions get clinically checked at the front and released into the workflow. Everything scans. The system flags the exceptions. The team handles the volume.

He gets his weekends back. He's building five consultation rooms. He's invested in independent prescribers who can run their own clinics because they're not tied to final checking.

The old photos are still in the drawer. Good to remember where it started.

#TitanPMR #PharmacyLife #IndependentPharmacy #PharmacyOwner #MalpasPharmacy
posts.name: TITAN_Geoff_PhotoDump_LI

posts.post_caption:
"It's a no brainer. Like, why haven't you done it already?"

That is Emma. Dispensing manager at Malpas Brook.

The board is what changed things.

Every prescription visible. Every stage. Still with the doctor. Part dispensed. Which basket. Which bay. Exactly where in the building.

Patients come in. They are not waiting around. They get an answer. Straightaway.

💬 Does your whole team know exactly where every prescription is right now?

#TitanPMR #CommunityPharmacy #PharmacyWorkflow #Newport
posts.name: TITAN_Emma_Malpas_NoBrainer_LI

posts.idea:
This is the hero post of the week. Let the content do the heavy lifting. The caption should not repeat the whole script. It should frame the bigger point:

pharmacies do not need more admin discipline
they need better infrastructure

That’s the real argument underneath the feature.

So Wednesday is the mechanism post:
what changes when repeat ordering moves into the system instead of living in inboxes, spreadsheets, sticky notes and one team member’s memory.
posts.post_caption:
The problem isn't that pharmacies are disorganised.

It's that repeat prescriptions were never designed to live in a system.

They live in inboxes. In spreadsheets. In someone's memory. You add discipline. You add reminders. You add another person to chase.

None of that changes the infrastructure.

Titan Repeat moves the whole workflow into the PMR.

Requests come in. Items are tracked. Surgeries are chased automatically. Nothing waits on one person.

Not a workaround. A system.

#TitanPMR #TitanRepeat #CommunityPharmacy #PharmacyWorkflow

Visualise the gap, not the solution. Three stages of a patient journey laid out horizontally:

CONSULTATION — [VOID] — NEXT APPOINTMENT

Stage one (Consultation) is solid, lit, real. The middle section is the visual tension: a long empty space with a broken line, labelled simply — 'This is where patients disappear.' Stage three (Next Appointment) is faded, ghosted — uncertain.

No emoji. No bullet points. Pure typography and geometry. The emptiness is the point.

posts.post_caption:
The consultation went well.

The patient left.

Then nothing.

No confirmation message.
No follow-up.
No pre-consult before the next appointment.

Pharmacy puts everything into getting patients through the door. Then hands them off to silence.

That is not continuity of care. That is a one-touch service dressed up as something more.

The value of a clinical service is not the consultation.

It is everything that happens around it.

#Titanverse #ClinicalPharmacy #CommunityPharmacy #PatientCare #PharmacyServices

"So we were already running clinics across both branches using other systems or making our own structure. But what we didn't realize with Titanverse was how everything was under one roof. We could send a message to a patient to confirm their appointment. We can then send a follow up message. Questionnaires were built into it so we could get pre consults from the patient. It meant that we could essentially create a far more streamlined service for patients, allowing us to actually save more time and actually increase the capacity. Previously we might have thought, okay, we could only do maybe five consultations in an hour, and now we can do more. It also allows us to scale up services better."

Then cuts to (12:37):
"Titanverse is a game changer. Having something linked to your PMR means that you don't have to have multiple tabs open. You have it all under one system."

SUGGESTED EDIT:
• Open on "What we didn't realise with Titanverse was how everything was under one roof"
• Cut through the message/questionnaire/pre-consult section
• Land on "Previously we could only do five consultations in an hour, now we can do more"
• End on "Titanverse is a game changer" as the close

ALT B-ROLL OPTION (5:03–6:05): Rahul explains multi-user logins, compliance, technicians running services, PGDs going across to Titan dashboard. Good for overlay footage if needed.

End card: Titanverse logo + titanverse.co.uk

posts.post_caption:
"We were wasting time chasing repeats before Titan. Now that time goes into services and revenue."

That's Rahul at Puri Pharmacy.

Here's what repeat management looks like without a system:

- Confirm a date with the patient
- Calculate when the order needs to go to the surgery
- File it away and remember to pull it in time
- Fax it or type out an email
- Wait for the prescription to come back
- Manually reconcile what the surgery sent
- Chase anything that's missing

Every patient. Every time.

Now after the first entry, Titan handles the whole thing automatically. Orders go out on time. Prescriptions get reconciled before the pharmacist sees them. Missing items get flagged without anyone remembering to check.

The team only touches it when there's a genuine clinical reason to.

"If your repeats rely on memory and chasing, you don't have a system. You have a bottleneck."

Same team. Different system.

#TitanPMR #CommunityPharmacy #PharmacyLife #RepeatPrescriptions

posts.idea:
A pain-led static post.


Angle: repeat prescriptions should not depend on one person, a spreadsheet, and pure memory.


This is the recognition post. It should speak to the pharmacies where repeats live in someone’s head, where people are chasing surgeries, checking spreadsheets, and hoping nothing gets missed.


A simple quote-style visual or clean graphic is enough. Don’t do a carousel. Don’t over-explain it.


A decent hook would be:


If your repeat system falls apart when one person is off, it isn’t a system.


That’s sharp, clear, and sets up Wednesday nicely.
posts.post_caption:
Some repeat systems have been seriously makeshift.

And you’ve got to give pharmacies credit.

A spreadsheet.
A reminders list.
An email chasing a missing item.
And then the repeat person goes on holiday.

And that’s just one workaround. Most pharmacies have several.

It feels archaic because it is.

Titan Repeat exists to replace that patchwork with a proper system.

Learn more: bit.ly/4seaaOD

#TitanPMR #TitanRepeat #CommunityPharmacy #PharmacyWorkflow
posts.name: TITAN_Repeat_Pain

posts.post_caption:
Most people think service growth comes from hiring more staff.

But often, it starts by removing the stress that is swallowing the team you already have.

That was the shift here.

“Now we have a less stressful environment.”

And that changed what the team could actually do.

Ear wax removal.
BP checks.
ABPMs.

Before Titan, they were struggling to do LMSs.

Now they’ve gone from 5 or 6 a month to 60 or 65 a month.

Even blood pressure checks are hitting 62 to 70 a month.

Same pharmacy.
Same team.
Different system.

Service growth did not start with adding more people.

It started with creating the space for the team to do more than just survive the dispensary.

That is what Titan changes.

#TitanPMR #CommunityPharmacy #PharmacyServices
posts.name: Pritee - Most people think service growth comes from hiring more staff.

posts.post_caption:
Most people talk about dispensing errors like they’re a staffing problem.

More checks. More pressure. More people trying to be more careful.

But that misses the point.

Because when near misses keep happening, it usually is not because the team does not care.

It is because the system is asking human beings to hold too much together.

That was the reality for Asif on ProScript.

“We would have between 5 to 6 near misses a month easily on ProScript.”

Same pharmacy.
Same responsibility.
Different system.

“I’ve had 2 since we’ve had Titan… and that’s in a year. And they were human errors”

That is the shift.

The problem was never effort.
The problem was the workflow.

“Now I have 0 worries. Honestly it’s the best thing that’s happened.”

Good pharmacy software should not just save clicks.
It should make the pharmacy safer to run.

Titan does that.

#TitanPMR #CommunityPharmacy #PatientSafety

posts.post_caption:
People still think of pharmacies as chemists.

But inside many of them, something very different is happening.

They are quietly becoming clinical diagnostic hubs.

Pharmacists are not just dispensing anymore.
They are running consultations.
Triaging conditions.
Delivering treatment.

Sounds like progress.

But here is the problem.

Most clinical services are still held together with five or six disconnected tools.

Booking platforms that do not talk to consultation software
Paper SOPs and policies
WhatsApp for governance
Spreadsheets for reporting

Too many systems.
Too much typing.
Nothing actually connected.

And this is the part nobody talks about.

The shift to clinical pharmacy is not the hard part.

Scaling it is.

What determines whether clinical pharmacy scales is not just demand, training or ambition.

It is the infrastructure behind it.

That is what Titanverse is built for.

Connecting the moving parts of clinical services so pharmacists can practise at the top of their licence, not buried in admin.

posts.post_caption:
All pharmacists know that feeling.

You’re in the consultation… but you’re not really in it.

Rahul put it plainly.

“If I’m being totally honest with you… you’re either typing something, not looking at the patient, or you’re scribbling things down.”

“Note-taking was, in fairness, pretty poor.”

He was already running clinics across two branches. Already using systems. Already building structure around them.

What he didn’t realise was how much friction sat between them.

When Titan, Titanverse and demdx came together properly, everything moved under one roof.

Pre-consult questionnaires built in.
Appointment confirmations sent automatically.
Follow-ups handled inside the same system.

And then the shift that changed the room:

AI transcription.

“It’s just saved probably about 10 to 20 minutes per patient.”

Not rough notes.
Not something to tidy up later.
High-quality documentation done in the background.

“Now we can maintain good eye contact with the patient, build a really good rapport.”

The result?

Double the consultations.
Higher standard of care.
Safer environment for the patient.

“People still regard pharmacies as chemists where we’re now clinical diagnostic hubs.”

That leap doesn’t happen because you work harder.

It happens when your systems finally work together.

#Titanverse #CommunityPharmacy #ClinicalServices #PharmacyBusiness
posts.name: Rahul - 2x Consultations and Improved Quality

posts.idea: SERVICES GOVERNANCE — GPhC INSPECTION READINESS (TV Theme 3: Compliance Burden / Theme 8: Scale Problem / Theme 14: Central Control). Single image already designed — shows four Titanverse governance modules: Audit Logs, Role Access, Compliance Module, SOP Management. Dark background with Titanverse pink-magenta accents. 'All in one place' headline. Image is ready to publish as-is. Caption uses fear-based hook (GPhC inspector scenario) crossed with specific scenario retention mechanics to make pharmacy owners feel the compliance gap before presenting Titanverse as the built-in solution. Tier 2 — makes pharmacy owners see themselves in the 'Sarah handles it' line. Voice register: Expert (Register 2) with peer-level specificity.
posts.post_caption:
A GPhC inspector walks into your pharmacy tomorrow.

Not to check your dispensary. To check your services.

Who ran last Thursday's travel clinic? Were they signed off to do it? Can you show the SOP they followed? Has every member of your team acknowledged the updated prescribing protocol?

If your answer involves opening a shared drive, scrolling through WhatsApp, or asking Sarah because she "usually handles that stuff"...

That's not a process. That's a liability.

With the new supervision rules, you're finally out of the dispensary. But that means your governance has to be bulletproof while you're gone.

Private prescribing, consultations, travel clinics, aesthetics. If it happens in your pharmacy, it's under your professional accountability.

So we built governance directly into Titanverse.

Every consultation logged. Every role defined with the right permissions. Every SOP read, signed, and tracked. One compliance score across your entire services operation.

Not a folder. Not a workaround. Built in.

Get ready to be compliant and register your interest today 👉 bit.ly/4ryqIAh

#CommunityPharmacy #PharmacyServices #Titanverse #PharmacyGovernance
posts.name: TV_ServicesGovernance

posts.idea:
Video clip of Sajid speaking to camera. This is existing footage already posted to LinkedIn previously, now repurposed as a short-form clip for the Titan page.

Clip focus: Sajid's quote about pharmacists getting to go home on time, not working weekends, enjoying family life. The emotional core is the human impact, not the product.

Edit style: Clean cut of the key section. Subtitles throughout (white text, dark background bar). Titan PMR logo watermark bottom corner. No music bed needed, Sajid's delivery carries it.

Key transcript section to feature:
"It's pharmacists just saying like, I get to go home on time. I don't have to work weekends. I don't need to come in early. These are the stories what we're all looking at because, at the end of the day, business is business, but at the end of the day, you wanna enjoy your family life as well."

If the full clip is too long for LinkedIn optimal (90-120 seconds), trim to the strongest 60 seconds focusing on the 'go home on time' section.
posts.post_caption:
Some things matter more than growth in pharmacy.

We see it every day with our customers.

Pharmacists finishing at 5. Owners who haven't worked a Saturday in months. Parents making it home for bedtime instead of locking up at 8pm.

And the best part? They actually get both.

Owners doing 15,000 items a month who still leave on time. Businesses scaling without the person behind them burning out.

But what drives Sajid more than anything, going pharmacy to pharmacy, seeing Titan customers up close, isn't the volume. It isn't the efficiency.

It's a pharmacist, a father of four, saying: I get to go home on time. I don't have to work weekends. I get to enjoy my family.

Business is business. But you didn't get into pharmacy to miss your life.

#CommunityPharmacy #PharmacyTech #IndependentPharmacy
posts.name: TITAN_SajidGoHomeOnTime

posts.idea: OBSERVATION POST — NO CUSTOMER QUOTE, NO PRODUCT MENTION IN GRAPHIC. Single image, editorial feel. Headline text: Five tools. Five logins. Five points of failure. White text on dark background. No Titanverse UI, no product screenshots. This is an observation post that names the pain without pitching the solution. Titanverse logo subtle bottom corner only. Design should feel like an industry editorial, not a software vendor ad. Positioning: Fragmentation Problem (TV Theme 2). Makes status quo visible. Audience connects the dots themselves.
posts.post_caption:
How many subscriptions do you pay for to run one consultation?

Because more than one is already too many.

A booking platform.
A consultation tool.
A separate prescribing system.
Paper risk assessments.
Then re-entering everything back into the PMR.

Five. Six. Seven logins.
More tabs.
More chances for something to slip through.

And pharmacists wonder why scaling services feels impossible.

It is not a motivation problem.
It is not a skills problem.
It is not even a time problem.

It is an infrastructure problem.

The pharmacy industry built incredible clinicians
and then handed them a patchwork of tools that were never designed to work together.

Until that changes,
“just do more services” will keep sounding simple
and feeling impossible.

That is exactly what Titanverse was built to fix.
One platform for bookings, consultations, prescribing, and compliance.

#PharmacyServices #ClinicalServices #CommunityPharmacy
posts.name: TV_FragmentationObservation

Warm cinematic background continues. Three real customer quotes styled as movie reviews with star ratings and first names:

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ — "Thanks to Titan PMR, I'm probably still in community pharmacy. Otherwise I would have left a long time ago." — Prab

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ — "It's like a second pair of eyes." — Malia

Layout: Stack the three quotes vertically like pull-quote review cards. Each quote in italics with the customer name right-aligned beneath. Star ratings above each quote. Titan PMR logo + titanpmr.com at the bottom.

Optional: Add a "NOW SHOWING AT A PHARMACY NEAR YOU" line above the quotes to keep the movie theme alive.

DESIGN NOTES:
- Consistent warm golden/rose colour grade across all slides
- Bold cinematic serif typography for titles (think movie poster fonts)
- Taglines in lighter weight beneath
- Illustrated/silhouette style — NOT photographic, NOT stock photos
- Each slide must work as a standalone image if shared individually
- Keep text readable at mobile size
- Titan PMR logo subtle on slides 1-6, prominent on slide 7
- Slide 7 review quotes should feel like genuine movie critic pull-quotes — use quotation marks, italic text, and clean spacing

TONE: Funny, warm, self-aware. Every joke is a real Titan AI benefit in disguise. Product education wrapped in entertainment. Final slide grounds the humour in real customer proof.

posts.idea:
ANIMATED MATRIX — Semrush-inspired grid format (see reference screenshots)

Single looping animation that cycles through three states. As the active tab changes, the corresponding tiles highlight in teal.

---

LAYOUT (consistent throughout):
- Header: large text — "One service. Five services. Full clinical model."
- Subheader: "Same platform. Different starting points."
- Three tabs across top: [Single Service] | [Multi-Service] | [Full Clinical Model]
- Grid of capability tiles below (4 columns x 4 rows):
  Row 1: Booking | Consultations | AI Scribe | Payments
  Row 2: NMS Workflow | Follow-Ups | Compliance | Reporting
  Row 3: Prescribing | Travel Health | Central Control | PharmAppy Integration
  Row 4: Staff Management | Patient Comms | Clinical Pathways | Multi-Site View
- Titanverse logo bottom right, titanverse.co.uk bottom left

---

STATE 1: "Single Service" tab active (teal)
Highlighted tiles: Booking, Consultations, NMS Workflow, Payments
All other tiles: dark grey (#2a2a3a), white text

STATE 2: "Multi-Service" tab active (teal)
Highlighted tiles: Booking, Consultations, AI Scribe, Payments, NMS Workflow, Follow-Ups, Compliance, Reporting, Travel Health, Clinical Pathways
All other tiles: dark grey

STATE 3: "Full Clinical Model" tab active (teal)
ALL 16 tiles highlighted in teal
Subtext appears at bottom: "One platform. Whatever you need. Whenever you're ready."

---

ANIMATION NOTES:
- Loop: State 1 → State 2 → State 3 → pause → restart
- Each state holds for ~2-3 seconds
- Tiles transition smoothly (fade or colour shift, not instant snap)
- Tab indicator shifts cleanly between states
- Total loop: ~8-10 seconds

---

DESIGN NOTES:
- Dark background (#0d0d1a or similar)
- Teal highlight: Titanverse brand teal
- Grey tiles: #2a2a3a with white text
- Clean, modern grid — rounded corners on tiles
- Reference: Semrush SEO/AEO matrix post (screenshots provided)
- Keep tiles compact — 2-3 words max per tile
- No icons needed, text-only tiles work best for this formata

posts.idea:
Carousel Script (7 slides):
SLIDE 1 (Hook):

£158 MILLION CLAWBACK

Announced Dec 23rd.
Two days before Christmas.
Quietly slipped out.

Here's what you need to know.
SLIDE 2 (What is clawback?):

WHAT IS CLAWBACK?

The CPCF sets an "allowed profit envelope" for pharmacy.

If the sector's profit exceeds this level, the government claws it back the following year.

Sounds fair?

Keep reading.
SLIDE 3 (The unfair bit):

HERE'S THE CATCH

Clawback is calculated on the WHOLE INDUSTRY.

Not individual pharmacies.

If Boots makes excess profit, your pharmacy pays too.

Even if you're dispensing at a loss.
SLIDE 4 (The numbers):

THE REALITY

- £16.8m clawed back per quarter
- Starting January 2026
- Applied to Category M and A drugs
- No individual assessment
- No appeals process
SLIDE 5 (The context):

THE BIGGER PICTURE

- 63% of pharmacies at risk of closure
- 248 closed in 2024 alone
- <10,000 remain in England
- 60% of owners took no salary in 2024
- £2.6bn funding gap

And now: clawback.
SLIDE 6 (The quote):

"Pharmacies cannot plan, cannot invest, and are forced to dispense NHS medicines, day after day, often at a loss."

– Olivier Picard, NPA Chair
SLIDE 7 (CTA):

WHAT CAN YOU DO?

1. Understand your exposure
2. Track affected drug categories
3. Make the opaque visible

Titan can help.

titanpmr.com
Visual Brief:
• Bold, stark design
• Use red/warning colour accents
• Large numbers on each slide
• Clean, impactful typography
• NPA quote on dedicated slide
• Titan positioning subtle - focused on industry advocacy first
Notes:
• This is "editorial/oracle" positioning - Titan being the voice of pharmacy
• Pairs anger/injustice with practical help (reporting)
• Follows the Mounjaro pricing post formula (timely + affects wallets + emotional)
• Can link to a future "how Titan helps with clawback tracking" follow-up post

posts.post_caption:
Technician supervision has already begun in UK pharmacy.

This legal change is a good thing because it reflects what already happens in most community pharmacies.

Pharmacists check the prescription, authorise it and seal the medication.

When the patient arrives, the handout is often completed by a trained member of the pharmacy team.

The new law simply formalises this workflow.

Titan supports this transition.
Titan’s workflow shows who checked the item and when it was authorised.
Titan Mobile adds clinical continuity by prompting the team with the pharmacist’s notes at handout, such as NMS reminders or key counselling points.

This gives pharmacies a safe, structured way to delegate handout while keeping the pharmacist’s clinical guidance in the process.

hashtag#CommunityPharmacy hashtag#TechnicianSupervision hashtag#PharmacyPractice hashtag#TitanPMR

posts.post_caption:
Bulk nomination is undermining patient choice.

And it has created a completely unfair playing field.

Some PMR systems allow pharmacies to switch patients back in bulk, without consent and without a conversation. Patients have no idea their nomination has changed behind their backs.

Pharmacies who refuse to do this lose business.
Pharmacies who do it gain business.
That isn’t fair competition. It’s a broken system.

Bulk switching goes directly against patient choice.
And it punishes the pharmacies who try to do the right thing.

There are only two legitimate scenarios for a bulk nomination change:
• When a pharmacy is sold
• When two pharmacies merge
And even then, the NHS manages the change — not the PMR system.

Everywhere else, nomination should stay where it belongs:
with the patient.

If you agree this needs to stop, take a stand with us.

👉 Sign the petition: bit.ly/3YH8boA
posts.name: Bulk nomination is undermining patient choice.

posts.post_caption:
Safy’s Chemist was losing time every single day.

They were handling 14,500 to 15,000 items a month, and they felt the pace.

Baskets piling up.
Running between rooms.
Checking, bagging and shelving in between appointments.
Back and forth all day.

For Asif, that rhythm felt normal for a busy pharmacy.

Titan changed that completely.

He now spends about an hour in the dispensary across a full day.
Around 85 percent of his time is spent outside it.
The team runs the process.
He can sit with patients.
People mention it in their reviews ⭐

Safety changed as well.
On ProScript, they had five or six near misses a month.
Since moving to Titan, they have had two in an entire year.
Both were human errors, not system issues.

As Asif puts it:
“Now I have zero worries. Honestly, it’s the best thing that’s happened.”

His story shows something many pharmacies overlook.
You are losing more time than you think.
Once that changes, everything else follows.

posts.post_caption:
Partial systems create full-scale problems.

Human triage burns people out. Digital triage scales 🔄

In most pharmacies, people are the system.
They carry the priority 🧠

When the system cannot surface what matters, everything turns into ad hoc triage.

One person decides what is urgent.
One person remembers what is next.
One person holds the risk ⚠️

It works for a while.
Then the volume hits.

Interruptions spike.
Signals get missed.
Fatigue creeps in 😮‍💨

It does not fail instantly.
It fails at scale.

The risk does not disappear.
It lands on the people trying to hold everything together.

And when complexity keeps landing on humans, the cost shows up fast:

• throughput drops ⏳
• stress climbs 😵‍💫
• confidence slips
• growth stalls 📉

You cannot try harder out of this.

You need systems that think before people have to.

When priority and logic live inside the infrastructure, not in someone’s head, you stop relying on heroics and start building something that can actually scale 💡

See how TITAN removes the hero burden → bit.ly/3Nk6VW2

posts.post_caption:
The patient comes first. 

Stop nomination ping-pong.

The NHS Electronic Prescription Service is one of the most advanced prescribing systems in the world. It works because patient nomination is meant to be clear, deliberate, and respected.

But there is a flaw. Some systems can automatically reverse a patient’s pharmacy nomination, without the patient knowing.

When that happens, prescriptions can bounce between pharmacies. Patients lose clarity about where their medicines are. Trust takes the hit first. And pharmacies playing fair are put at a disadvantage.

At scale, this creates risk for EPS itself. It was not designed for businesses to fight over nominations through automation.

We were asked to build this functionality. We refused. Not because we couldn’t, but because it breaches our values around patient safety, professional standards, and fair competition.

Patient choice must mean something.

👉 Sign the petition: bit.ly/3YH8boA

#PatientChoice #EPS #NHSPharmacy #PharmacyEthics #PatientCare #CommunityPharmacy

posts.post_caption:
Most pharmacies hit a ceiling.

At around 4,000 items a month, the team already feels at capacity.
That’s where Sagar’s pharmacy started.

Today, they safely process around 17,000 items a month.
And even at that volume, the team stays calm, collected and in control.

The difference is the workflow.

Sagar explains it simply:
“Titan AI gives me the right tools to do my clinical checks… and it saves me time.”

He runs Titan AI to support those checks, reducing the manual work he used to do every day.

Batch flow streamlines the dispensing process.
And the Kanban view gives a clear audit trail, showing exactly what stage each prescription is at.

The impact?
Sagar now spends around 80% of his time on services instead of checking in the dispensary.

The data tells the story: 4,000 → 17,000 items.
A smoother, more organised way of working.

If one pharmacy can scale like this without losing control, the question is simple:

👉 What is stopping your pharmacy from doing the same?

Read the full story: bit.ly/3KLwE95

#PharmacyGrowth #PharmacyScaling #TitanPMR #DigitalPharmacy #PharmacyEfficiency #CommunityPharmacy #PharmacyInnovation

posts.post_caption:
Independent Prescribing should be the future.

So why will it struggle in 2026?

Every branch is training more IPs. Every policy document points to a more clinical role. But in reality, most pharmacists still start their day the same way they did ten years ago: clearing repeats, chasing stock and firefighting the bench.

Pharmacy First showed the demand is there.
What is missing is capacity.

Training more prescribers cannot overcome a workflow that gives them no time to prescribe.

Until the operational model changes, IP cannot scale.
The bottleneck is not skill. It is time.

👉 This carousel outlines the problem
👉 Full breakdown in the blog (link in comments)

posts.post_caption:
Every year in England, 237 million medication errors occur.
(BMJ & WHO data)

Most do not cause harm.
But some do.
And every single one adds stress, cost, and risk.

The best safeguard is not more pressure on pharmacists.
It is smarter systems that:

• Reduce human error
• Build safety checks into every stage
• Free teams to focus where they are needed most

Safe pharmacy is efficient pharmacy.
And efficient pharmacy is what patients deserve.

👉 bit.ly/45wM9bo

#CommunityPharmacy #PharmacyOwner #PharmacyLife #PharmacySafety #DigitalPharmacy #FutureOfPharmacy

posts.post_caption:
Another incredible independent pharmacist shaping the future of community care ✨

👉 Meet Gareth Hughes

Gareth spent years in senior roles across large organisations, but something was missing.
He wanted real patient contact.
He wanted to give back to a community he could feel connected to.
He wanted face-to-face conversations, not dashboards and performance figures.

So he made the move into independent community pharmacy.
And what happened next is a story worth reading.

From service delivery to business growth to the decisions that shaped his workflow, Gareth has transformed Tynewydd Pharmacy with a clear vision and a commitment to genuine patient care.

He won Independent Pharmacist of the Year at the Independent Pharmacy Awards.

A well-earned moment for a pharmacist who chose people over spreadsheets and built a practice that genuinely serves his community.

👉 See the full story in the carousel

#CommunityPharmacy #IndependentPharmacy #PharmacyServices #PharmacyLeadership

posts.post_caption:
Spot the difference. 

The GTIN doesn’t.

Right now in UK pharmacy, different medicines are arriving with the same GTIN barcode or a GTIN that does not match the product inside.

When that happens, the scanner passes the wrong medicine as correct. Every safety layer collapses.

Pharmacists are forced to slow down, override the check, and carry the risk that should sit with manufacturers and regulators.

This is not a workflow problem. It is a patient safety problem.

The fix is simple:
• Make accurate GTIN barcodes mandatory
• Treat incorrect GTINs as labelling errors
• Protect the final safety check that keeps patients safe

If you work in pharmacy, please add your name.
This is a small action that protects every dispenser in the country.

👉 Sign the petition: bit.ly/3XNxapM

#EveryPackCounts #PharmacySafety #GTIN #CommunityPharmacy #TitanPMR

Carousel Slide Plan (7 slides)



Slide 1 — Hook
Headline: The Growth Trap
Text: More items. More staff. More stress.
Image: Visual of a snare trap or tangled ropes labelled “growth.”


Slide 2 — The Trap Explained
Headline: Why Hiring Feels Like Progress
Text: More people means you can handle more items — for a while.
Image: Pharmacy team expanding, crowded workspace.


Slide 3 — The Breaking Point
Headline: Why It Breaks
Text: Costs rise. Margins shrink. Teams burn out.
Image: Balance scales tipping under “wages” and “stress.”


Slide 4 — The Smarter Path
Headline: Workflows That Scale
Text: Titan clears bottlenecks so you can do more with the same team.
Image: Calm, flowing process diagram.


Slide 5 — Proof
Headline: What Changes With Titan
Bullets:

• 70% prescriptions AI-checked
• 150 baskets managed at once with Batch Flow
• Same staff, more services delivered
Image: Clean infographic icons.



Slide 6 — Human Impact
Headline: Growth Without Burnout
Text: More items, more services, more calm. That is sustainable growth.
Image: Pharmacist with patients, smiling team.


Slide 7 — CTA / Belonging
Headline: Escape The Growth Trap
Text: Smarter scaling starts with smarter workflows.

Image: Confident pharmacist looking ahead.

posts.post_caption:
❗ Most pharmacies now have trained Independent Prescribers.

Most of them still cannot prescribe.

Not because pharmacists are unwilling.
Not because the training is flawed.
Not because IP is a bad idea.

Something far simpler is blocking it.

Every owner we spoke to knows exactly what it is.
Every superintendent has lived it.
Every IP has felt it.

This new piece breaks down the real reason Independent Prescribing stalls inside most pharmacies and why the NHS 10 Year Plan will not land unless this changes.

If you run a pharmacy, you will recognise every line.

🔗 Read the full piece: https://bit.ly/4ibkzX0

#Pharmacy #IndependentPrescribing #CommunityPharmacy #PharmacyFirst #ClinicalPharmacy #TitanPMR

posts.post_caption:
170,000 items a month sounds impressive.

Living it is something else. 👀

Yusuf knew BMP HEALTHCARE LTD could not keep absorbing that pressure.
Too much checking. Too much noise. Too many days shaped by the bench.

Then he saw Titan at the Pharmacy Show in 2023.
Something clicked. ⚡
Not hype. Not promises.
A clear path to a calmer, faster operation.

So they rebuilt.
Pharmacists moved into consultation rooms.
Scanning became second nature.
Checks fell to around 20 to 30 percent.
Services grew by hundreds of percent.
The pressure lifted. 💬

This is the video where Yusuf talks through that shift.
What changed.
What surprised him.
And why he will not go back.

📖 Full written story → bit.ly/4nOpOgu

#TitanPMR #PharmacyLeadership #PharmacyWorkflow #DigitalPharmacy #AIinPharmacy #CommunityPharmacy

posts.post_caption:
Pharmacy is moving fast.
That’s why we move faster.

Most PMR systems release new updates a few times a year.
At Titan, we do it every week.

Every release is shaped by:
🔹 Customer feedback
🔹 Live performance data
🔹 Conversations with the people behind the bench

We’re on release 44 this year, with 45 coming next week.
Each one includes small improvements that make a big difference.

Because pharmacy doesn’t wait, and neither do we.

#TitanPMR #PharmacyInnovation #PharmacyTechnology #DigitalPharmacy #PharmacyLife

posts.post_caption:
Inside Tariq’s Pharmacy Show Talk.
The message was clear.
Dispensing alone isn’t enough anymore.

At The Pharmacy Show 2025, Tariq Muhammad shared what many in the room already felt.
The model that once made pharmacy stable is now under strain.

Funding is flat. Volume keeps rising.
But the future isn’t doom and gloom.
It’s in taking control.

From TITAN’s survey of 95 pharmacies:
📊 86% plan to grow private services.
📈 59% still want to expand NHS delivery.

Pharmacy’s direction of travel is clear.
Independent. Efficient. Service-led.

If you missed the talk, this carousel shares the key messages that resonated most.
You can also read the full piece here:
bit.ly/43eKMO2

Book a demo at titanpmr.com
Join the waiting list at titanverse.co.uk

#PharmacyShow2025 #TitanPMR #CommunityPharmacy #PharmacyInnovation

posts.post_caption:
One of our favourite things about The Pharmacy Show is hearing real stories of change. 💬


This one stopped us in our tracks 👇


💷 From a £70,000 loss to thriving success
🏥 30,000 items a month
👥 Fewer staff, more time
💉 New services like travel vaccines and ID Pathfinder


Mr Walker from Lytham Road Pharmacy summed it up perfectly:


“Titan has made a massive difference. We’re now making a good profit, and the team has time to focus on services.”


Hearing stories like this is what makes The Pharmacy Show so special.


📍 Lytham Road Pharmacy | The Pharmacy Show 2025
🌟 titanpmr.com | titanverse.co.uk


#TitanPMR #Titanverse #PharmacyShow2025 #PharmacyInnovation #CommunityPharmacy #DigitalHealth #PharmacyTransformation

posts.idea:
Slide 1 (Cover):
“The pharmacist stepped out… and everything stopped.”
(Visual: a paused pharmacy scene. Clock ticking. Dispensers waiting.)


Slide 2:
Tea break?
Consultation room?
Speaking to a patient?
In most pharmacies, the whole system freezes without the pharmacist.


Slide 3:
That’s not normal.
That’s not fair.
And it’s fixable.


Slide 4:
With the right workflow and AI tools, dispensers don’t have to wait.
They move forward.
They run the dispensing show.


Slide 5:
Paracetamol doesn’t need a full clinical review every single time.
The system knows.
And that’s how the team flows.


Slide 6:
Happy patients.
Fewer delays.
And yes. Everyone gets to go home on time.


Slide 7 (Final CTA):
Dispensers aren’t the problem. The system is.
Let’s change it.
[titanpmr.com/join-us]

posts.post_caption:
Hub and spoke. Before the law changes.

Medichem Pharmacy shows how.

Most contractors are waiting for October 2025 to see if hub and spoke will work for independents.
But waiting could mean falling behind.

Jeet Vadodaria did not wait.
At Medichem Pharmacy, he built a central hub, connected his four branches with Titan P2P, and reimagined what his team could do.

The results:
✅ Dispensers four times more efficient
✅ 45% less stockholding across branches
✅ Pharmacists free to focus on services
✅ Patients walk into calmer, safer pharmacies

“Peer to peer is a brand new tool, which Titan has enabled us to use at the minute. And I think it’s amazing, because if you are part of the same group, you could technically consider sending your prescriptions to be dispensed at your other branch.” – Jeet Vadodaria

This October, hub and spoke becomes open to every independent.
Medichem proves it can work, safely and profitably, with patients at the centre.

👇 Will your pharmacy be ready?

📖 Full case study: [insert link]

posts.idea:
Hub and Spoke: What Changes, What To Do, What To Watch



Image text: From 1 Oct 2025, independents can use hubs across ownership lines.
Caption (ally voice):
Here’s the short version for owners:
• Law in force: 1 Oct 2025
• To start: notify your ICB at least 28 days before you begin
• If you notify on 1 Oct and there is no objection, earliest start is 29 Oct 2025
Checklist: hub due-diligence, SOPs, courier SLAs, business continuity, data sharing, audit trail.
Bottom line: plan now. Put patient safety and resilience first.
CTA: What is the one risk you will mitigate first? Add it below so others can sanity-check their plan.
posts.post_caption:
Your pharmacy’s workflow could change forever.

From 1 Oct 2025, independents can use hubs across ownership lines.

Here’s what owners need to know:
1️⃣ Law in force: 1 Oct 2025
2️⃣ Notify your ICB: at least 28 days before you begin
3️⃣ Earliest start: If you notify on 1 Oct and there’s no objection → 29 Oct 2025

Before you begin, check off:

• ✔ Hub due diligence
• ✔ SOPs
• ✔ Courier SLAs
• ✔ Business continuity
• ✔ Data sharing
• ✔ Audit trail

Bottom line:
Plan now. Patient safety and business resilience come first.

👉 What’s the first risk you’ll tackle? Share below so others can sanity-check their plan.
posts.name: Hub-and-Spoke: What Actually Changes on 1 Oct?

posts.post_caption:
Fast isn’t the same as efficient.

Pharmacy shouldn’t feel like a Fast & Furious sequel.
Flying labels. Exploding tokens. Teams racing flat out…
and still falling behind.

You can’t just go faster, harder, longer.
Because the pile doesn’t shrink. It just shifts.

The problem isn’t your people.
It’s the system.

Most teams aren’t failing.
They’re firefighting.

With Titan, everything changes:
✅ Tasks routed to the right person automatically
✅ Pharmacists see only high-risk scripts
✅ Accuracy checks run in real time
✅ Everyone knows exactly what to do, when to do it

No more double handling. No more bottlenecks.
Just calm, steady flow.

posts.idea: TBD
posts.post_caption:
Fast isn’t the same as efficient.

Pharmacy shouldn’t feel like a Fast & Furious sequel.
Flying labels. Exploding tokens. Teams racing flat out…
and still falling behind.

You can’t just go faster, harder, longer.
Because the pile doesn’t shrink. It just shifts.

The problem isn’t your people.
It’s the system.

Most teams aren’t failing.
They’re firefighting.

With Titan, everything changes:
✅ Tasks routed to the right person automatically
✅ Pharmacists see only high-risk scripts
✅ Accuracy checks run in real time
✅ Everyone knows exactly what to do, when to do it

No more double handling. No more bottlenecks.
Just calm, steady flow.

So the team you already have?
They finally get the space to do their best work.

Where could your team shine if the system stopped getting in their way?

#PharmacyLeadership #WorkflowDesign #DigitalTransformation #TeamWellbeing #ClinicalSafety #TitanPMR
posts.name: Your team doesn’t need to work harder. Your system does.

posts.post_caption:
Sure, Teslas can drive themselves.

But pharmacists cannot swap a pill.

That is where we are in 2025.

Patients are waiting up to three weeks for essential medicines.
Not because shelves are empty.
But because pharmacists are legally blocked from substituting.

The result:
👉 Patients left untreated
👉 Surgeries drowning in calls
👉 Pharmacies stuck in the firing line

The absurdity is obvious.
We trust machines to steer cars through traffic.
But not pharmacists to use their training when a drug is out of stock.

The CCA and others are pushing for substitution rights, deprescribing roles and more front line flexibility. The NHS 10 Year Plan points the same way.

The question is not whether pharmacy is ready.
It is whether the rules will catch up.

#pharmacy #NHS #shortages #patientcare #TitanPMR
posts.name: 3 Weeks No Meds

posts.post_caption:
In most PMRs, safety depends on signatures and memory.
In Titan, it’s automatic.

Every script has a full digital audit trail:
✅ Who clinically checked it
✅ When it was labelled
✅ When it was handed out
✅ Who handed it out

No guesswork. No missing scripts. No “did we give that out?” moments.

For teams, it means less stress and fewer mistakes.
For pharmacists, it means freedom to focus on services.
For patients, it means faster, safer handouts.

That’s what pharmacy should feel like. Calm. Clear. Accountable.

👉 Follow us for more ways Titan is changing pharmacy.

#CommunityPharmacy #PharmacyOwner #FutureOfPharmacy #DigitalPharmacy #TitanPMR #AIinHealthcare

posts.post_caption:
💥 What if dispensing didn’t eat up every hour of the day?

This is Faz, dispenser at Howletts Pharmacy in Ruislip.
And in this brilliant tutorial, she shows exactly how Titan Batch Flow works in practice:

✔ QR-coded baskets
✔ Every item scanned, every time
✔ Split packs flagged automatically
✔ Dispensing up to 15 baskets at once

The result?
Less back-and-forth.
Less stress on the pharmacist.
More time in the diary for services and patients.

Thank you Faz for walking us through it 🙌
And thank you Pritee, for building a pharmacy culture where digital tools free your team to shine.

👉 Read the full Howletts Pharmacy case study: bit.ly/45yhU53

#pharmacy #communitypharmacy #TitanPMR #pmrreimagined #pharmacylife
posts.name: Faz - 💥 What if dispensing didn’t eat up every hour of the day?

posts.post_caption:
💥 What was the Mounjaro Bomb that hit UK pharmacy last week?

Here’s what really changed 👇

From 1 September, Mounjaro’s list price jumps by up to 170%.

But the real shock isn’t just the cost. Pharmacies must now:
1️⃣ Pay the higher price upfront
2️⃣ File monthly rebate reports (splitting NHS vs private)
3️⃣ Wait for retrospective rebates — still unclear in detail

For independents running on tight margins, that’s a cashflow crisis.
For patients, it risks confusion, delays, and unsafe online alternatives.

That’s why within days, Titan PMR launched a custom reporting suite — now used by 1,000+ pharmacies to:
✔ Generate rebate data in minutes
✔ Stay compliant without drowning in admin
✔ Keep the focus on patients, not paperwork

When global shocks land in local pharmacy…
👉 Titan moves fast.

#pharmacy #mounjaro #pmrreimagined #TitanPMR

posts.post_caption:
If dispensing still eats every hour of your day…

what happens to your services, your patients, your future?

That’s the question Pritee asked herself at Howletts Pharmacy in Ruislip.

Before Titan, she was buried in baskets. Accuracy checks stacked high. End-of-month claims dragging into days. Services struggling to take off.

Now?
Her team runs calmly and confidently:

• 6,500 items a month
• 50–60% prescriptions AI-checked
• 20–30% faster dispensing with Batch Flow

That freed up space for real growth:
✔ 60–70 BP checks a month (up from 10–15)
✔ 18 active weight management clients
✔ 2–3 private consultations every day

And for Pritee, the most important shift is cultural:

“Technology is wonderful, but it can’t replace people. Titan frees my staff up to be there for the community — and that’s what matters most.”

This is what calm, digital-first pharmacy looks like.
👉 Read the full case study: bit.ly/45yhU53

#pharmacy #communitypharmacy #pharmacist #pharmacylife #independentpharmacy #digitalhealth #healthtech #AIinHealthcare #pharmacyservices #futureofpharmacy #TitanPMR #pmrreimagined

posts.post_caption:
Most pharmacy systems were built for paperwork.

Tick boxes. Admin checks. Spreadsheets.

But here’s the truth:
Clinical pharmacy needs more than admin support.
It needs digital tools that think clinically.

That means:
⚡ Filtering high-risk items before they reach you
⚡ Flagging interactions and dose changes in real time
⚡ Reducing the mental noise so you can focus on people
⚡ Checking every label, every time
⚡ Adapting instantly as patients change

If your system isn’t doing this, it isn’t clinical-ready.

Pharmacy is moving fast. The tools you choose now will decide whether you keep up — or fall behind.

👉 See the 5 signs in full, then ask: is your digital system really clinical-ready?
titanpmr.com/join-us

#Pharmacy #DigitalHealth #ClinicalSafety #PharmacyOwner #TitanPMR
posts.name: Digital is now clinical.

posts.post_caption:
If you want to see the future of independent pharmacy ✨

you need to see what Jeet, Yamini and their team are building.

We joined them in Leeds. First for a meal at one of their favourite spots 🍴

Then for a tour of their hub and spoke operation where we visited the central warehouse and one of the spoke pharmacies powered by Titan P2P 🏭💊

We will not spoil the full story just yet 👀 but here is what we can say.
This team is proving how independents can scale safely, efficiently and without compromise.

Big thanks to Jeet, Yamini and the whole team for the hospitality, the curry and the glimpse of what is possible with Titan 🙏

posts.post_caption:
Sunshine. Laughter. Basically a school sports day.

Titan BBQ 25 had it all.

Once a year, we get the whole Titan crew together for BBQ 25. A day to swap the laptops for laughter, the deadlines for a bit of good-natured rivalry, and remember what it is like to be properly outside.

🥥 Coconut shy
⚽ Football speed tests
💪 Tug of war
🏃 Sack races (with some questionable technique)
🍔 And food worth queueing twice for

It had all the magic of being back on the school field, just with fewer grazed knees and much better catering.

Thank you to every single one of you for bringing the energy, the humour and the team spirit that made BBQ 25 what it was. You turned it into more than just a day out, you made it a day to remember.

Here is to BBQ 26. Same time next year. Same team.

#TitanBBQ25 #TeamTitan #SummerCelebration
posts.name: Titan BBQ 25 had it all.

posts.post_caption:
📄 Last year, Rahul’s pharmacy was frantically using paper diaries, post it notes and prescriptions everywhere. Scribbling everywhere.

Every day was busy but without structure.
Prescriptions were hard to track.
Services were squeezed.
The bench ruled everything.

Fast forward to today.
Rahul is running 18,000 items a month across two branches without chaos.

📦 Repeat flow reorders all MDS patients with one click.
🤖 Titan AI screens prescriptions and clears around 70% automatically.
📲 Automated texts tell patients their meds are ready.
⏱ 20 pharmacist hours a week saved.

Those hours now go into Pharmacy First, weight loss and medical cannabis clinics.

In his own words:

“I am not glued to the bench. We are now a community health hub.”

Rahul’s story is not just about efficiency.
It is about creating the headroom to lead clinically, commercially and creatively.

📖 Read Rahul’s full case study here
👉 bit.ly/408lMqm

#Pharmacy #CommunityPharmacy #DigitalHealth #PharmacyAutomation #PharmacyFirst #IndependentPharmacy #PharmacyTech #CaseStudy #PharmacyWorkflow #TitanPMR

posts.post_caption:
The NHS is transforming.

Digital-first. Clinically led. Built around community and prevention.

The 10-Year Plan sets out a clear vision:

• Cloud-based systems
• Clinical AI
• Service-first pharmacy
• Integrated, paperless records

But while others plan for the shift…
Titan already delivers it.

✅ Cloud-native
✅ Paperless workflows
✅ AI-supported checks

No overtime. No stress. Just a system that works, and a team that runs with it.

It’s not about doing more.

posts.post_caption:
Titan checks every prescription. Every time.

Let us explain why.

Because that “routine repeat” from January?
Your patient’s changed since then.
New meds.
New risks.
New reasons to recheck.

And yet…
Most systems still only check every 6 or 12 months.
It sounds efficient.
But it isn’t safe.

Titan’s different.

✔ High-risk → straight to the pharmacist
✔ Low-risk → checked by AI trained on pharmacists
✔ Barcode → confirms item, label, and patient match

Not about checking less.
About checking smarter.

Because safety isn’t static.
And neither is your patient.

📍 titanpmr.com/join-us
#PatientSafety #ClinicalCheck #DigitalHealth #PharmacyWorkflow

posts.post_caption:
🚀 How Sagar 4x Pharmacy Items

and spends 80% of his time on services

✅ No chaos
✅ No overtime
✅ No dispensary firefighting

Just a calm, efficient system built right from the start.

When Sagar took over Brighton Hill Pharmacy, it was doing 4,000 items a month.
Now it’s doing 17,000.

He didn’t add staff. He added space. For clinics. For services. For growth.

Here’s what made it possible:

💡 AI handles most clinical checks
📦 Every script is tracked from inbox to collection
📲 Patients get a text when it’s ready
⏱️ Same-day turnaround is the norm

He spends one hour a day in the dispensary.
The rest goes into services: travel clinics, contraception, ear care, weight loss.

🧑‍⚕️ He even built a second consultation room.
Sagar isn’t just scaling.
He’s scaling with ease. He’s leading by example.

📖 Read the full story → bit.ly/4lOuT7S

👏 Congratulations to Sagar and the team at Brighton Hill Pharmacy.

💬 What would you do with 80 percent of your time back?

#CommunityPharmacy #PharmacyServices #DigitalHealth #NHSPrimaryCare #TitanPMR #PharmacyInnovation

posts.post_caption:
When everyone discovers your favourite band… 

years after you.

That’s what this month has felt like.

AI in UK pharmacy finally got national attention.

The MHRA launched a £1 million AI sandbox to test health tech safely.
NHS England published a 10-Year Plan to build the world’s most AI-enabled healthcare system.

It’s being called a bold new beginning.

But here’s what most people miss:
Some pharmacies are already there.

Since 2021, AI has been live in real, high-volume UK sites.
Not in beta. Not on paper. Just quietly working.

✅ Clinical checks completed in seconds
✅ Real-time risk flagging before issues happen
✅ Pharmacists free to focus on services, not admin

AI isn’t on the horizon
It’s already reshaping what pharmacy feels like

Calmer. Faster. More clinical

⏳ Deadline: 30 June 2025

📉 Miss it, and your governance rating will be flagged as “Inadequate.”

Why it matters:
✅ Required for NHS funding
✅ Needed for new pharmacy services
❌ Mandatory under the NHS contract

Even if you submitted last year, you must resubmit.

🧠 Get guidance here → bit.ly/44hlbUC

posts.post_caption:
Pharmacy’s future isn’t about dispensing.

It’s about stepping into the clinical frontline 🩺

And patients are already on board.

📊 A 2024 NHS survey found pharmacists are one of the most trusted healthcare professionals in the UK.

But you can’t deliver clinical care if you’re buried in admin.
Not with a PMR that makes you do everything twice.
Not with systems that interrupt you 50 times a day.

To make this shift work, we need to fix three things:
⚙️ Workflow
💸 Reimbursement
📋 Regulation

Automation plays a critical role — not to replace pharmacists, but to reclaim human time and free up headspace for clinical care.

Step out of survival mode.
Step into something better.
👉 Read how: https://bit.ly/3FBQgKp

#CommunityPharmacy #PharmacyWorkflow #PharmacyFirst #ClinicalPharmacist #UKPharmacy #PharmacyInnovation #PrimaryCare #NHSPharmacy

posts.post_caption:
Some pharmacies might walk away from Pharmacy First

And it’s not hard to see why

New rules mean if you don’t hit every target, you lose up to £1,000/month

That includes:
✔ Minor illness consultations
✔ Contraception services
✔ Hypertension checks
⚠️ And soon: 1 ABPM per month

The problem?
Only 30% of pharmacies currently meet the full bundle
ABPM is slow, low demand, and hard to convert in a walk-in model
Miss it—and you lose everything

No flexibility
No partial credit
No margin for real-world pharmacy

This is how NHS dentistry broke
And pharmacy could be next

With pressures this high, more owners are asking:
Should we be focusing on private services instead?

What needs to change before October?
The targets? The model? Or the direction altogether?

Add your voice to the conversation below

#PharmacyFirst #CommunityPharmacy #PharmacyFunding #ABPM #PharmacyServices #PharmacyPressure #FutureOfPharmacy #TitanPMR

posts.post_caption:
🚨 Pharmacies just ranked #1 for healthcare access.

Meanwhile?
GPs were 640% more likely to be called “hard to reach.”

New YouGov data confirms what pharmacy teams already know:
They show up — when others can’t.

Let’s break it down:

• Just 5% said pharmacies were hard to access
• For GPs? 37% — the worst of any setting
• Pharmacies ranked #1 overall — ahead of GPs, dentists, A&E and hospitals
• And when patients couldn’t get a GP appointment, most said it harmed their health

Despite rising pressure, pharmacies stayed open, trusted, and accessible.
And the public noticed:

• 77% would be concerned if their pharmacy closed
• 65% said pharmacies are personally important
• Most now want pharmacies to do even more clinical care

📌 Pharmacies aren’t a fallback. They’re the front door to the NHS.

If you believe that too — share this.

#CommunityPharmacy #PharmacyFirst #PrimaryCare #NHS #HealthcareAccess #TitanPMR #ThankYouPharmacy

📅 What’s Changing in the Pharmacy Contract — And When

From fee uplifts to service requirements, the next 12 months are a big shift for community pharmacy.

✅ SAF rises to £1.46
✅ Monthly banded payments begin
✅ ABPM + contraception targets introduced
✅ Depression added to NMS
✅ EHC joins the contraception service
✅ PQS claim window + payments confirmed

We’ve broken it all down — month by month — in a clear, no-fluff carousel.

Whether you’re running one branch or twenty, this is the timeline to watch.

posts.post_caption:
Even the best pharmacies make errors — it’s human nature.
So, how do we reduce the risk?

We train our teams to double-check.
Triple-check.
Stay alert at every step.

But constant vigilance isn’t sustainable — it leads to exhaustion, not excellence.

We often say:
“Be careful.”
“Double-check everything.”
“Don’t miss a thing.”

But here’s the real question:
Why are we still designing systems that assume people won’t make mistakes?

The safest workflows don’t rely on perfect memory or superhuman focus.
They’re built to catch errors automatically.

🧾 Every label
💊 Every item
📄 Every prescription

500 near misses prevented every single day
Not by luck — by design.

Because true safety doesn’t come from trying harder.
It comes from building better systems.

🗣️ Let’s move the industry forward
Share your thoughts in the comments
Tag a pharmacy leader who needs to see this
Or pass it on to your team

#PharmacySafety #TheTitanWay #DigitalPharmacy #PharmacyAutomation #SmarterWorkflows #PMRThatDelivers #PatientSafety #PharmacyLeadership

posts.post_caption:
🚨 URGENT: Independent Pharmacies Are at Risk 🚨

More closures are coming. Unless we take action now!

MPs need to hear directly from you about the funding crisis threatening independent pharmacies. Every email sent increases pressure on Parliament to act.

Follow these 3 simple steps to make them listen:

🛑 Step 1: Find Your MP
📍 Use this link: members.parliament.uk/members/commons

✉️ Step 2: Send an Email
📩 Use our template (see comments) or write your own

📧 Step 3: CC IPCN & Key MPs
Make sure to CC IPCN: office@ipcn.org.uk

Also, CC the following key MPs:
📌 Wes Streeting (Health Secretary): dhsc.publicenquiries@dhsc.gov.uk
📌 Rachel Reeves (Chancellor): public.enquiries@hmtreasury.gov.uk
📌 Keir Starmer (Prime Minister): keir.starmer.mp@parliament.uk

🚨 This is urgent. Every email counts. 

Find your MP, send your email and share this with other pharmacy owners.

Let’s push for change!

#PharmacyCrisis #SaveCommunityPharmacy #PharmacyFunding #IndependentPharmacy #PharmacyAdvocacy

posts.post_caption:
A Proud Moment for Titan PMR – EPS is Now Fully Rolled Out Across Wales

On 17th November 2023, Titan PMR made history.

We became the first PMR to deploy EPS in Wales, with Wellington Road Pharmacy in Rhyl leading the way.

Patients there were the first in Wales to experience a fully digital prescription process.

Now, EPS is fully rolled out across all Titan pharmacies in Wales, marking a major milestone in our mission to transform pharmacy care.

What does this mean for pharmacies?

✅ No more paper prescriptions → Fully digital workflow.
✅ Faster, safer dispensing → Fewer errors, better stock control.
✅ Less admin, more efficiency → Simplified reimbursements & operations.

💬 "Visiting Wellington Road Pharmacy, I witnessed firsthand the benefits of the Electronic Prescription Service, which allows GPs to send prescriptions securely online to the patient's choice of community pharmacy, eliminating the need for paper forms."
— Eluned Morgan MS, Minister for Health and Social Services

💡 This is just the beginning. At Titan PMR, we’re committed to driving the future of pharmacy innovation.

🔗 Visit titanpmr.com to see how we’re shaping what’s next.

#EPSWales #PharmacyEfficiency #DigitalPharmacy #PharmacyInnovation #BetterPatientCare

posts.post_caption:
Pharmacies can boost revenue and patient care.

Here’s how they’re doing it

Pharmacies in the UK are stepping up as GPs face increasing demand. The upside? Every consultation brings extra NHS income for your pharmacy.

But here’s the challenge:
Most pharmacy teams are stretched thin managing dispensing and day-to-day operations. To qualify for Pharmacy First consultation payments, you need to handle at least 25 consultations a month.

That’s where automation and AI make the difference. Tools like Titan streamline workflows, automate admin tasks, and free up your team to focus on patients.

The results:
• More consultations, more revenue.
• Faster, smarter patient care.
• A future-ready pharmacy that thrives under pressure.

With the right systems, you can meet rising demand, grow private services, and keep your pharmacy running smoothly.

Sure you have.

SLIDE 2 – Problem / Fear (Dip Down)

Copy: 
It’s not a controlled drug.
Not a risky interaction.
Just… a dressing.

But the system doesn’t know that.
So you check it anyway.

Visual suggestion:
A pharmacist staring at a screen showing a dressing prescription. Slightly slumped posture. Maybe multiple low-risk scripts stacked nearby.

Copy:
Titan filters first.
High-risk scripts go to pharmacists.
Low-risk ones — like dressings — go to AI.

if you weren’t checking dressings.
